title: askellia pygmaea subsp. ramosa
intro: Askellia pygmaea subsp. ramosa, also known as the alpine anemone, is a plant species classified within the genus Askellia, characterized by its small size and delicate appearance. Traditionally, the alpine anemone has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing, although further research is necessary to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the alpine anemone is often used in infusions or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
